title: Climate-Temperature-Forecasting: A Reproducible Multimodal Machine Learning Pipeline for Global Surface Temperature Prediction
description:
Software and data supplement for:

Choudhary, N. & Kulkarni, S.A. (2026). 'Global Temperature Forecasting Using Multimodal Machine Learning: An Empirical Study Integrating CO2 and Solar Activity Data.' Climatic Change (Springer). Under review.

This repository contains:
- 4 Jupyter notebooks (data preprocessing, EDA, model training, evaluation)
- 9 Python source modules (preprocessing, feature engineering, 5 model trainers, evaluation, ensemble, prediction)
- 23 paper figures (9 EDA + 14 results)
- 4 CSV result tables (literature review, dataset summary, hyperparameters, 9-model benchmark)
- Trained model weights (Keras + pickle)
- Full conda/pip environment specifications

Best model: CNN-LSTM (MAE=0.0944 degC, RMSE=0.1175 degC, R2=0.6781, Murphy Skill=0.7462 on test set 2018-2025).
Decadal ensemble forecast (2025-2035): mean 1.25 degC - below the Paris Agreement 1.5 degC threshold.

creators.name: Choudhary, Neeraj
creators.affiliation: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune, India

creators.name: Kulkarni, Sheetal Abhijit
creators.affiliation: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune, India

notes:
REPRODUCING RESULTS:
1. Run notebooks in order: 01 -> 02 -> 03 -> 04
2. Datasets fetched automatically from public APIs
3. Random seed = 42 throughout
4. Expected CNN-LSTM test MAE: 0.0944 +/- 0.003 degC

FIGURE INDEX:
- results/figures/eda/ : 9 EDA figures (Figs 1-6 in paper)
- results/figures/     : 14 result figures (Figs 7-13 in paper)

TABLES:
- results/tables/table1_literature_review.csv
- results/tables/table2_dataset_summary.csv
- results/tables/table3_hyperparameter_config.csv
- results/tables/table4_benchmark_results.csv
